metadata.credit: Data and analysis provided by Draftsim
metadata.author: Bryan Hohns (Veveil)

set_overview.summary: Shadows over Innistrad Remastered is an Arena-exclusive set combining cards from Shadows over Innistrad, Eldritch Moon, and older Innistrad block sets (Innistrad, Dark Ascension, Avacyn Restored). The "Shadows of the Past" slot rotates thematic card pools weekly over the month-long availability.

color_notes.{B} Black: Worst commons with lots of duds (Merciless Resolve).

key_gameplay_notes: Set is softer on archetypes than most sets — six of the signposts are monocolor with off-color costs.
Bombs > strong general playables > speculative archetype payoffs > archetype filler > general filler.
Splashing is possible but not prominent; Simic Clues and Golgari Delirium are best splash candidates.
Commons matter — the gulf between commons and uncommons feels small.
Don't force decks based on the weekly Shadows of the Past theme; solid fundamentals beat theme exploitation.

mechanics.name: Madness
mechanics.summary: Cast discarded cards from exile, often at a reduced cost. Primarily Rakdos.

mechanics.name: Delirium
mechanics.summary: Cards trigger powerful effects when 4+ card types are in your graveyard. Primarily Golgari.

mechanics.name: Emerge
mechanics.summary: Sacrifice a creature to reduce the casting cost of a spell by the creature's mana value. Multicolor with Simic emphasis.
mechanics.performance: Strong modal play — cheap with setup or hard cast late.

mechanics.name: Clues / Investigate
mechanics.summary: Create Clue tokens you can sacrifice for two mana to draw a card. Blue/green primarily.

mechanics.name: Transform
mechanics.summary: Double-faced cards flip based on spell-cast conditions or mana payments.
mechanics.performance: Universal — every color has examples; emphasis Red/Green.

key_examples.name: Ulvenwald Captive
key_examples.type_line: Creature — Werewolf Horror // Creature — Eldrazi Werewolf

mechanics.name: Meld
mechanics.summary: Specific card pairs combine into a single powerful card under conditions. Rare; high payoff when assembled.

tier_a.name: Azorius Spirits (Tempo)
tier_a.strategy: Tempo deck built around spirits with fliers. Uses Nebelgast Herald and Essence Flux for disruption.

tier_a.name: Simic Clues / Emerge (Midrange-Control)
tier_a.strategy: Grind value through Clue tokens and emerge synergies.

tier_a.name: Rakdos Madness (Tempo-Aggro)
tier_a.strategy: Discard-madness synergy with a vampire tribal element.

tier_a.name: Golgari Delirium (Midrange)
tier_a.strategy: Enable delirium through the 4+ card type graveyard threshold. Sample card-type balance: 11 creatures, 3 artifacts, 3 enchantments, 3 instants, 3 sorceries, 17 lands.

tier_b.name: Dimir Zombies (Tribal Midrange)
tier_b.strategy: Tribal zombie payoffs; heavily dependent on rare cards.

tier_b.name: Izzet Spells (Tempo-Control)
tier_b.strategy: Two distinct builds. Slow: Rise from the Tides + removal/draw. Fast: Mercurial Geists with pump spells and untap synergies.

tier_b.name: Gruul Wolves (Midrange-Aggro)
tier_b.strategy: Tribal wolves with midrange/aggro positioning. Payoffs are variable; high trap potential.

key_cards.name: Geier Reach Bandit
key_cards.type_line: Creature — Human Rogue Werewolf // Creature — Werewolf

key_cards.name: Duskwatch Recruiter
key_cards.type_line: Creature — Human Warrior Werewolf // Creature — Werewolf

tier_c.name: Boros Aggro
tier_c.strategy: Attack-focused aggro; no compelling synergies beyond creature quality. "Play them, curve out, and hope to draft a more fun deck next time."

tier_c.name: Orzhov Midrange
tier_c.strategy: Good-rate creatures and removal with a balanced curve; a slower Boros. Card advantage is hard to come by.

bomb_rares.name: Archangel Avacyn
bomb_rares.type_line: Legendary Creature — Angel // Legendary Creature — Angel

general_tips: Follow fundamentals — commons matter and the gulf to uncommons is small.
Don't force based on the weekly Shadows of the Past theme; respect signals.
Simic Clues and Golgari Delirium are the best splash candidates.
Bomb rares (Avacyn, Tireless Tracker, Ishkanah, Diregraf Colossus, Sorin) are too good to pass.
Use mana fixing (Terrarion, Wild-Field Scarecrow, Weirding Wood) for flexibility.
